search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae adjustment disorder stress telehealth guideline PDF
site:who.int conditions specifically related to stress guideline PDF
site:doh.gov.ae acute stress reaction guideline UAE
rejection_reasons: Workplace wellness and opinion pages are not clinical guidance.
Patient handouts do not support clinician documentation.
Questionnaire output must not create an autonomous diagnosis.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for an identifiable psychosocial stressor and trauma-related acute stress; not exact for every generic stress, burnout, panic, or dissociative presentation.
setting_applicability: Direct for DHA telehealth mood, stressor, safety, support, mental-status, and clinician-led follow-up documentation plus WHO non-specialist trauma-related assessment.
UAE_applicability: DHA depression and adjustment content is direct for Dubai telehealth. WHO trauma-related content requires local pathway review.
recency_verification: Current DHA Issue 2 depression guidance and the WHO stress-related conditions guideline were reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: No newer official DHA depression issue or replacement WHO stress-related conditions guideline was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports anxiety, low mood, sleep, concentration, function, medical or medication history, stressor, and support context.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports low mood, loss of interest, sleep, appetite, energy, concentration, guilt or worthlessness, duration, function, previous episodes and treatment, medical and medication history, stressors, social context, comorbidity, mania or hypomania, substance use, family history, and support assessment.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports clinician-assessed suicide, self-harm, bipolar, psychotic, substance, mental-status, function, support, and risk fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician assessment of suicide, self-harm, harm or neglect, protective and support factors, mental status, cognition, insight, substance use, medical contributors, medication history, thyroid testing, and selected blood testing when indicated.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports clinician-confirmed suicide, self-harm, severe-function, and safety warning prompts.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists suicidal ideation or attempt, risk of harm or neglect, moderate or severe depression, and psychotic or bipolar features as escalation concerns.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports stressor and adjustment terminology plus event, situational, social, and occupational context without making a diagnosis.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Describes depressed mood in response to an identifiable psychosocial stressor and supports documenting single, multiple, recurrent, or continuing social and occupational stressors.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports clinician-documented supportive discussion, safety planning, questions, and arranged follow-up without autonomous treatment.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented supportive discussion, questions, safety planning, treatment or medication decisions, monitoring, follow-up, and referral without autonomous treatment selection or doses.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports acute traumatic-stress terminology and trauma-exposure context only when a potentially traumatic event and relevant symptoms are present.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines acute traumatic stress through intrusion, avoidance, hyperarousal, and significant functional impairment after a potentially traumatic event and supports clinician-led psychological-first-aid or trauma-focused care documentation without medication invention.

unresolved_source_gaps: Panic symptoms, dissociation, work or school-note issuance, fixed one-to-two-week review, broad duration presets, and the combined multi-section history draft remain unsupported.
The workflow must not infer adjustment disorder, acute stress disorder, depression, PTSD, treatment, medicine, therapy referral, sick leave, or risk level.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
